title: Adult Nurse
how_to_become: To become an adult nurse in the NHS, complete a pre-registration degree programme accredited by the Nursing and Midwifery Council (NMC).
description: Adult nurses have the specialist knowledge and skills to provide care to adults from the age of 18 onwards. They use a wide range of skills to influence, deliver, plan, and assess a person's care and treatment, and play a vital role in empowering patients to make informed decisions about their care and wellbeing.
role_overview: As an adult registered nurse, you'll be a key member of the healthcare team, delivering direct care and support and making complex clinical decisions. You'll be responsible for people's physical, clinical, and emotional needs across a variety of specialities.
work_experience: Work placements and volunteering can provide healthcare experience. Contact your local health board to find out about opportunities.

undergraduate_universities: University of the Highlands and Islands
University of the West of Scotland
education_and_training.postgraduate_duration: 3 years full-time. Requires an undergraduate degree in any subject.
education_and_training.open_university: 4-year part-time BSc combining nursing support worker role with online study. Scottish Government covers all tuition fees.
education_and_training.hnc_route: HNC in Healthcare Practice at SCQF level 7 — a stepping stone for healthcare support workers, may allow entry to year 2 of a nursing degree.
education_and_training.certhe_route: CertHE in Healthcare Practice at SCQF level 7 — for nursing support workers, progressing to assistant practitioner or nursing degree.
education_and_training.swap_route: SWAP — one-year access course for adult learners out of education for 3+ years, preparing for entry to a partnered university nursing degree.
education_and_training.return_to_practice: Former NMC-registered nurses can return via a return to practice course (14 to 26 weeks) or the NMC Test of Competence.
education_and_training.bursary: Paramedic, Nursing and Midwifery Student Bursary (PNMSB) — £10,000 non-repayable bursary for eligible students ordinarily residing in Scotland.

learning_and_development.overview: Once registered, there are ongoing education and skills development requirements. Flying Start programme provides extra support in the first year.
learning_and_development.revalidation: Nurses must revalidate with the NMC every 3 years.

professional_bodies: Nursing and Midwifery Council (NMC) — mandatory registration

chunks.text: To become an adult nurse in the NHS, complete a pre-registration degree programme accredited by the NMC.

Adult nurses provide care to adults from age 18 onwards, using a wide range of skills to influence, deliver, plan, and assess care and treatment, empowering patients to make informed decisions about their wellbeing.
Adult nurses deliver direct care, make complex clinical decisions, and are responsible for patients' physical, clinical, and emotional needs across a variety of specialities.
Useful school subjects include Biology, Chemistry, Psychology, English, and Maths.

10 Scottish universities offer NMC-approved undergraduate adult nursing degrees (3 years full-time). Postgraduate routes (3 years) are available at 4 universities for those with any undergraduate degree. Open University offers a 4-year part-time route. HNC Healthcare Practice, CertHE, and SWAP provide alternative access routes. A £10,000 PNMSB bursary is available.

Adult nurses can specialise in district nursing, general practice, emergency care, intensive care, cancer care, and more. Progression routes include senior nurse, advanced nurse practitioner, and consultant nurse.
Adult nurses must register with the NMC and revalidate every 3 years. They can also join the Royal College of Nursing (RCN).
